what: Bug Report & Fix: generate(max_calls) Accuracy Regression in rollama v0.5.0
title: Bug Report & Fix: generate(max_calls) Accuracy Regression in rollama v0.5.0
text: document mirrors equivalent bug report ollama-classifier v0.6.0. rollama reproduces conceptual flaw (inherited v0.5.0 port Python library) applies fix.

title: Bug Report & Fix: generate(max_calls) Accuracy Regression in rollama v0.5.0
text: generate() method conceptual flaw adaptive cluster-resolution algorithm. max_calls > 1, supplementary API calls constrained model subset labels, placing logprobs different probability space initial full-label call. incompatible logprobs mixed single geometric mean, producing invalid scores monotonically decreased accuracy max_calls grew — confirmed experimentally Python reference implementation across 4 choice-configurations 4 call budgets (16 variations, 637 products ). Root cause: Mixing logprobs different constraint contexts. Fix: Reproportioning — supplementary calls redistribute probability mass within cluster, never changing -group totals.

title: Bug Report & Fix: generate(max_calls) Accuracy Regression in rollama v0.5.0
text: experiment (summary.txt, ollama-classifier v0.5.0, qwen2.5:3b-instruct) runs generate() max_calls ∈ {1, 3, 5, 8} across 4 choice-configurations.

what: Accuracy — monotonic decrease in ALL configurations (before fix)
title: Bug Report & Fix: generate(max_calls) Accuracy Regression in rollama v0.5.0
text: Every configuration shows monotonic accuracy decrease.

title: Bug Report & Fix: generate(max_calls) Accuracy Regression in rollama v0.5.0
text: original generate() used BFS loop resolve clusters:
code: frontier <- list(list(labels = labels, resolved_length = 0L)) while (length(frontier) > 0 && (is.null(max_calls) || calls_made < max_calls)) {   cluster <- frontier[[1]]   cluster_labels <- cluster$labels  # SUBSET of all labels!    response <- backend$chat(     constrain_labels = cluster_labels,  # ← Different constraint set!     ...   )   # Logprobs from this call were APPENDED into accumulated scores   # and fed into a single geometric mean }

title: Bug Report & Fix: generate(max_calls) Accuracy Regression in rollama v0.5.0
text: constraint set changes {, B, C} → {, B} → {B}, logprob distribution changes fundamentally: Post-mask logprobs (≈0.0) dilute negative signal genuine tokens geometric mean, artificially inflating scores labels many unscored tokens.

title: Bug Report & Fix: generate(max_calls) Accuracy Regression in rollama v0.5.0
text: Labels: =[shared, a_end] (2 tok), B=[shared, b_mid, b1, b2, b3] (5 tok) Model’s true preference: > B > C mc=1: B = geom_mean([-0.3, -0.6]) = -0.45 → wins ✓ mc=2: B = geom_mean([-0.3, -0.6, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0]) = -0.18 (inflated!) → B wins ✗

title: Bug Report & Fix: generate(max_calls) Accuracy Regression in rollama v0.5.0
text: Keep max_calls sole parameter (max_calls = 1 means resolution). Call 1 constrains model labels produces initial probability distribution using divergence-aware logprobs. logprobs come constraint context, distribution internally consistent. Calls 2…max_calls resolve clusters: groups ≥2 non-winning labels share scored prefix diverge winner. cluster: Make constrained call cluster’s labels Compute divergence-based relative weights (softmax geometric-mean scores) Reproportion: redistribute cluster’s total probability mass (summed initial distribution) according relative weights -group probabilities never changed. subset call’s absolute logprobs may shifted (post-mask), ratios matter reproportioning — ratios preserved renormalization.
code: P(group) from first call                # between groups, locked relative_weight(L) from subset softmax  # within group P_new(L) = P(group) × relative_weight(L) # hierarchical composition

title: Bug Report & Fix: generate(max_calls) Accuracy Regression in rollama v0.5.0
text: Accuracy never degrades increasing max_calls -group probability mass preserved — B+C mass stays constant Within-group probabilities reproportioned — B C shift relative Non-cluster labels unchanged — P() remains fixed Probabilities always sum 1.0

title: Bug Report & Fix: generate(max_calls) Accuracy Regression in rollama v0.5.0
text: Reproduced R via tests/testthat/test-classifier.R MockBackend:

title: Bug Report & Fix: generate(max_calls) Accuracy Regression in rollama v0.5.0
text: generate_fn(): 1. Single constrained call labels → initial probabilities 2. identify_unresolved_clusters() finds multi-label clusters shared prefixes 3. cluster (≥2 labels): subset call → relative weights → reproportion 4. Single-label clusters skipped (nothing reproportion) 5. Recursive: sub-clusters resolution added frontier accumulated per-label logprobs cluster member replaced (appended) subset call’s logprobs, used compute within-cluster relative weights.

title: Bug Report & Fix: generate(max_calls) Accuracy Regression in rollama v0.5.0
text: 54 tests pass (40 new classifier tests + 14 existing backend tests), including 3 regression tests ported ollama-classifier v0.6.0 TestMaxCallsMonotonicity: max_calls flip correct prediction: prediction stays correct max_calls reproportion preserves -group mass: ’s probability decrease max_calls single-token labels need resolution calls: single-token labels make exactly 1 call

title: Getting Started with rollama
code: # Install from GitHub # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("paluigi/rollama-classifier")

title: Getting Started with rollama
text: Ollama backend: Install Ollama (>=v0.12) pull model: Recommended models: llama3.2, llama3.1, mistral, qwen2.5.
code: ollama pull llama3.2

title: Getting Started with rollama
code: library(rollama)  backend <- ollama_backend(model = "llama3.2") classifier <- llm_classifier(backend)  result <- classify(   classifier,   text = "I love this product!",   choices = c("positive", "negative", "neutral") )  print(result$prediction) #> "positive" print(result$confidence) #> 0.85

title: Getting Started with rollama
code: backend <- vllm_backend("meta-llama/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct") classifier <- llm_classifier(backend)  result <- classify(   classifier,   text = "I love this product!",   choices = c("positive", "negative", "neutral") )

title: Getting Started with rollama
text: rollama offers two complementary scoring methods. return classification_result; differ spend API calls confidence computed. generate() makes one constrained calls. first call constrains model labels produces internally consistent probability distribution divergence-aware logprobs along winning generation path. Supplementary calls (max_calls > 1) resolve clusters labels share token prefix reproportioning probability mass within cluster — never change -group totals, accuracy never degrades call budget grows. result may approximate budget runs every label fully resolved. classify() scores every label independently via backend’s completion-scoring endpoint, applies softmax geometric-mean-normalized scores. always makes N calls N labels always exact.

title: Getting Started with rollama
text: max_calls argument controls generate()’s API budget. first call always made labels; supplementary calls reproportion probability mass within unresolved clusters:
code: # Fast, single-call prediction (may be approximate) result <- generate(classifier, text = txt, choices = choices, max_calls = 1) print(result$approximate) # TRUE if any label was only partially scored  # Adaptive: spend up to 3 calls to resolve ambiguities result <- generate(classifier, text = txt, choices = choices, max_calls = 3)  # Exact: resolve every label fully result <- generate(classifier, text = txt, choices = choices, max_calls = NULL)

title: Getting Started with rollama
text: generate() fully resolve label within call budget, label’s token logprobs partially known. Two fields report : result$coverage — named numeric vector giving fraction tokens scored per label (0.0 1.0). 1.0 means label fully resolved. result$approximate — TRUE label coverage 1.0. classify() always FALSE.
code: result <- generate(classifier, text = txt, choices = choices, max_calls = 1)  print(result$coverage) #>   positive    negative     neutral #>         1.0         0.5         1.0 print(result$approximate) #> TRUE print(result$n_calls) #> 1

title: Getting Started with rollama
text: Classify text predefined choices:
code: backend <- ollama_backend(model = "llama3.2") classifier <- llm_classifier(backend)  result <- classify(   classifier,   text = "The goalkeeper made an incredible save!",   choices = c("sports", "politics", "technology", "entertainment") )

title: Getting Started with rollama
text: Providing descriptions helps model understand category better:
code: choices <- list(   positive = "Text expresses happiness, satisfaction, or approval",   negative = "Text expresses anger, disappointment, or disapproval",   mixed = "Text contains both positive and negative sentiments",   neutral = "Text is factual without strong emotional content" )  result <- classify(   classifier,   text = "The food was amazing but the service was terrible.",   choices = choices )

title: Getting Started with rollama
text: Override default system prompt specialized tasks:
code: result <- classify(   classifier,   text = "The quarterly earnings exceeded analyst expectations.",   choices = c("bullish", "bearish", "neutral"),   system_prompt = "You are a financial sentiment analyzer.                    Classify financial news based on market sentiment." )

title: Getting Started with rollama
text: generate() returns classification_result just like classify(), reconstructed constrained generation:
code: result <- generate(   classifier,   text = "The team won the championship!",   choices = c("sports", "finance", "politics"),   max_calls = 1 )  print(result$prediction) print(result$confidence) print(result$method)      # "adaptive_generate" print(result$approximate) # TRUE if any label is partially scored

title: Getting Started with rollama
text: Classify multiple texts efficiently. batch variants return list classification_result objects:
code: texts <- c(   "The goalkeeper made an incredible save!",   "The central bank raised interest rates.",   "The new smartphone features a revolutionary camera." )  results <- batch_classify(   classifier,   texts = texts,   choices = c("sports", "finance", "technology") )  purrr::map2_chr(texts, results, ~ paste0(.x, " -> ", .y$prediction))

title: Getting Started with rollama
code: results <- batch_classify(   classifier,   texts = texts,   choices = c("bullish", "bearish", "neutral"),   system_prompt = "You are a financial sentiment analyzer." )

title: Getting Started with rollama
text: methods return structure:
code: result$prediction      # Character: predicted label result$confidence      # Numeric (0-1): confidence score result$probabilities   # Named numeric: probability distribution (sums to 1) result$method          # "adaptive_generate" or "multi_call" result$approximate     # Logical: TRUE if any label has partial coverage result$coverage        # Named numeric: per-label token coverage (0.0-1.0) result$n_calls         # Integer: number of API calls made result$raw_response    # List: raw data for debugging

title: Getting Started with rollama
text: package ships sample dataset 20 customer support tickets:
code: data(sample_tickets, package = "rollama") head(sample_tickets) #>                                                             text #> 1                          I was charged twice for my last order #> 2 Can I get a refund for the subscription I cancelled last week? #> 3       My invoice shows a different amount than what was quoted #> 4                           Where can I find my payment history? #> 5                          I need an update on my pending refund #> 6             The app keeps crashing when I try to upload a file #>      expected_label             label #> 1           billing           billing #> 2           billing           billing #> 3           billing           billing #> 4           billing           billing #> 5           billing           billing #> 6 technical_support technical_support #>                                                              label_description #> 1 Questions about charges, invoices, payments, refunds, and subscription costs #> 2 Questions about charges, invoices, payments, refunds, and subscription costs #> 3 Questions about charges, invoices, payments, refunds, and subscription costs #> 4 Questions about charges, invoices, payments, refunds, and subscription costs #> 5 Questions about charges, invoices, payments, refunds, and subscription costs #> 6           Issues with software, bugs, errors, login problems, or performance # Test classification accuracy results <- batch_classify(   classifier,   texts = sample_tickets$text,   choices = c("billing", "technical_support", "account", "general") )  predictions <- purrr::map_chr(results, "prediction") mean(predictions == sample_tickets$expected_label)

title: Inference Backends
text: rollama supports four inference backends, accessed llm_classifier() interface. backend created constructor function exposes unified set methods: chat(), score(), tokenize(). classifier talks backends common interface, generate(), classify(), batch variants work identically matter engine use. backends differ run constrain output valid labels.
code: library(rollama)  backend   <- ollama_backend("llama3.2")   # or vllm / sglang / llamacpp classifier <- llm_classifier(backend)

title: Inference Backends
text: Every backend constrains model’s generation one supplied labels generate() always produces valid choice. mechanism differs per engine, captured supports_bare_label_constraint flag: Backends supports_bare_label_constraint = TRUE emit label plain text. Ollama backend wraps label small JSON object ({"label": "<chosen>"}); rollama filters structural JSON tokens trie reconstruction uses context-dependent tokenization prefix trie matches actual response tokens. internal detail — paths feed generate() / classify() logic.

title: Inference Backends
text: backends share unified approach: tokenize() uses empirical forced constrained generation — forcing label valid choice chat() call reading back emitted value tokens. necessary standalone BPE tokenization produces different token boundaries model emits constraint guidance, break trie-based divergence scoring. Results memoized per label. Echo/prefill (vLLM, SGLang): /v1/completions echo=TRUE recover model’s genuine per-token logprobs label unexpected continuation prompt. /tokenize endpoint pinpoints label-token boundary. Forced constrained generation (Ollama, llama.cpp): forces label valid choice reads back model’s genuine per-token logprobs (teacher forcing). Used server support echo=TRUE (llama.cpp) fill---middle (Ollama).

title: Inference Backends
text: default backend. Uses native Ollama REST API (/api/chat). Requires Ollama runtime (>=v0.12) installed locally. Modern Ollama removed /api/tokenize endpoint support fill---middle (“insert”) instruct models. backend therefore obtains label tokenization completion scores empirical forced constrained generation: forces label valid choice chat() call reads back model’s genuine per-token logprobs. Tokenization results memoized per label amortize cost. Start: Connect: ollama_backend() defaults host = "http://localhost:11434". point remote Ollama instance, set host: Ollama constrains labels JSON Schema enum passed via format parameter, sets supports_bare_label_constraint = FALSE.
code: ollama pull llama3.2 ollama serve backend <- ollama_backend(model = "llama3.2") classifier <- llm_classifier(backend) backend <- ollama_backend(   model = "llama3.2",   host  = "https://my-ollama.example.com" )

title: Inference Backends
text: High-throughput serving engine LLMs. Communicates via OpenAI-compatible API supports structured_outputs.choice (vLLM v0.12.0+) logprobs box. score() uses echo/prefill (/v1/completions echo=TRUE) recover genuine per-label logprobs. tokenize() uses forced constrained generation via structured_outputs.choice token boundaries match actual constrained-generation output. Results memoized per label. Start local server: Connect: vLLM constrains labels natively structured_outputs.choice (replaces deprecated guided_choice removed vLLM v0.12.0), producing bare label text, supports_bare_label_constraint = TRUE. Remote server:
code: vllm serve Qwen/Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 backend <- vllm_backend(   model = "meta-llama/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct",   base_url = "http://localhost:8000/v1" ) classifier <- llm_classifier(backend) backend <- vllm_backend(   model = "your-model",   base_url = "https://your-vllm-server.com/v1",   api_key = "your-api-key" ) classifier <- llm_classifier(backend)

title: Inference Backends
text: Fast serving system large language models efficient radix attention. Also OpenAI-compatible. score() uses echo/prefill (/v1/completions echo=TRUE) recover genuine per-label logprobs. /tokenize endpoint (correct "prompt" field) pinpoints label-token boundary. tokenize() uses forced constrained generation via regex token boundaries match actual constrained-generation output. Results memoized per label. Start local server: Connect: SGLang constrains labels regex built label set ((label1|label2|...)), producing bare label text, supports_bare_label_constraint = TRUE.
code: python -m sglang.launch_server \     --model-path meta-llama/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct \     --host 0.0.0.0 --port 30000 backend <- sglang_backend(   model = "meta-llama/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct",   base_url = "http://localhost:30000/v1" ) classifier <- llm_classifier(backend)

title: Inference Backends
text: Lightweight inference via llama-server. Ideal CPU mixed CPU/GPU environments. score() tokenize() use forced constrained generation via GBNF grammar llama.cpp support echo=TRUE completions endpoint (returns generated-token logprobs, prompt tokens), echo/prefill approach used vLLM SGLang unavailable. Results memoized per label. Start local server: Connect: llama.cpp constrains labels GBNF grammar (root ::= "label1" | "label2" | "label3"), producing bare label text, supports_bare_label_constraint = TRUE. Note: Logprobs support requires llama-server compiled appropriate flag (e.g. LLAMA_SUPPORT_LOGPROBS). Constrained generation via grammar available recent llama.cpp builds.
code: ./llama-server -m model.gguf --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8080 -c 4096 backend <- llamacpp_backend(   model = "model",   base_url = "http://localhost:8080/v1" ) classifier <- llm_classifier(backend)

title: Inference Backends
text: backends share common configuration options: Ollama uses host instead base_url api_key parameter (native API needs none). backends use base_url api_key match OpenAI-compatible convention.

title: Inference Backends
text: llm_classifier() exposes API regardless backend use — constructor differs:
code: backends <- list(   ollama   = ollama_backend("llama3.2"),   vllm     = vllm_backend("my-model", base_url = "http://localhost:8000/v1"),   sglang   = sglang_backend("my-model", base_url = "http://localhost:30000/v1"),   llamacpp = llamacpp_backend("my-model", base_url = "http://localhost:8080/v1") )  purrr::imap(backends, ~ {   classifier <- llm_classifier(.x)   result <- classify(classifier, "Hello world!", choices = c("a", "b", "c"))   paste0(.y, ": ", result$prediction) })

title: Inference Backends
text: Every backend list unified methods plus capability metadata. primarily useful debugging advanced use:
code: backend <- ollama_backend("llama3.2")  backend$chat        # constrained chat completion backend$score       # completion scoring (used by classify()) backend$tokenize    # context-dependent tokenization backend$supports_bare_label_constraint  # FALSE for Ollama, TRUE elsewhere

what: Classify Text with LLMs on Ollama and Other Backends
title: Classify Text with LLMs on Ollama and Other Backends
text: Text classification LLMs using constrained output calibrated confidence scoring. Supports four inference backends single unified API: Ollama, vLLM, SGLang, llama.cpp. rollama offers two complementary scoring methods: generate() — Hierarchical constrained generation. single constrained call produces probability distribution labels using divergence-aware logprobs winning path. max_calls > 1, supplementary calls resolve label clusters reproportioning probability mass within cluster — never changing -group totals, accuracy never degrades call budget grows. classify() — Multi-call completion scoring. Scores every label independently geometric-mean normalization exact, calibrated probabilities. return classification_result prediction, confidence, full probability distribution, diagnostics.

title: Classify Text with LLMs on Ollama and Other Backends
text: Two scoring methods: adaptive generate() exact classify() Calibrated confidence: geometric-mean normalization eliminates length/concentration bias raw logprob summation Constrained output: every backend constrains generation valid labels (JSON enum, structured_outputs.choice, regex, GBNF grammar) Budget control: max_calls lets trade accuracy speed Unified backends: one llm_classifier() works Ollama, vLLM, SGLang, llama.cpp Flexible choices: simple labels labels descriptions Custom prompts: override default system prompt Batch processing: classify many texts

title: Classify Text with LLMs on Ollama and Other Backends
code: # Install from GitHub pak::pak("paluigi/rollama-classifier")  # Or with remotes remotes::install_github("paluigi/rollama-classifier")

title: Classify Text with LLMs on Ollama and Other Backends
text: need one inference server running locally remotely: Ollama (>=v0.12): Ollama installed running, model pulled (e.g., ollama pull llama3.2) vLLM: running vLLM server SGLang: running SGLang server llama.cpp: running llama.cpp server

title: Classify Text with LLMs on Ollama and Other Backends
text: rollama provides two scoring methods, returning classification_result. Pick based accuracy, latency, budget needs.

title: Classify Text with LLMs on Ollama and Other Backends
text: max_calls argument controls generate() spends API budget. first call always constrains model labels produces internally consistent probability distribution. Supplementary calls (max_calls > 1) reproportion probability mass within clusters unresolved labels — never change -group totals, increasing max_calls can improve leave unchanged prediction, never degrade . result approximate, inspect result$coverage (named numeric vector per-label token coverage, 0.0–1.0) result$approximate.
code: # Fast, single-call prediction (may be approximate) result <- generate(classifier, text = txt, choices = choices, max_calls = 1)  # Adaptive: spend up to 3 calls to resolve ambiguities result <- generate(classifier, text = txt, choices = choices, max_calls = 3)  # Exact: resolve every label fully result <- generate(classifier, text = txt, choices = choices, max_calls = NULL)

code: backends <- list(   ollama  = ollama_backend("llama3.2"),   vllm    = vllm_backend("my-model", base_url = "http://localhost:8000/v1"),   sglang  = sglang_backend("my-model", base_url = "http://localhost:30000/v1"),   llamacpp = llamacpp_backend("my-model", base_url = "http://localhost:8080/v1") )  purrr::imap(backends, ~ {   classifier <- llm_classifier(.x)   result <- classify(classifier, "Hello world!", choices = c("a", "b", "c"))   result$prediction })

title: Classify Text with LLMs on Ollama and Other Backends
text: generate() classify() (batch variants) return classification_result list:
code: result$prediction      # Character: predicted label result$confidence      # Numeric (0-1): confidence score result$probabilities   # Named numeric: probability distribution (sums to 1) result$method          # Character: "adaptive_generate" or "multi_call" result$approximate     # Logical: TRUE if any label has partial coverage result$coverage        # Named numeric: per-label token coverage (0.0-1.0) result$n_calls         # Integer: number of API calls made result$raw_response    # List: raw data for debugging

title: Classify Text with LLMs on Ollama and Other Backends
text: text (character): text classify texts (character vector): texts classify (batch methods) choices (character vector named list): labels, labels descriptions system_prompt (character NULL): optional custom system prompt max_calls (integer NULL, generate() ): API call budget — 1 (fast), K (adaptive), NULL (exact)

title: Inference backends for rollama — backends
text: backend provides unified interface chat(), score(), tokenize() methods, plus supports_bare_label_constraint capability flag. Backends communicate via HTTP using OpenAI-compatible API (vLLM, SGLang, llama.cpp) native API (Ollama).

title: Batch classification — batch_classify

Your task is to classify the given text into exactly one of the provided categories. Respond with only the category label, nothing else." #>  #> $user #> [1] "Classify the following text into one of these categories:

- positive
- negative
- neutral

Text to classify:
I love this!

Respond with only the category label." #>

title: Classification Result — classification_result
text: Result classification operation. Returned classify(), generate(), batch variants.

title: Classification Result — classification_result
code: classification_result(   prediction,   confidence,   probabilities,   method = "multi_call",   approximate = FALSE,   coverage = numeric(0),   n_calls = 1L,   raw_response = list() )

title: Classification Result — classification_result
text: prediction Character. predicted class label. confidence Numeric 0 1. Confidence score prediction. probabilities Named numeric vector. Probability distribution choices (sums 1). method Character. Scoring method used: "adaptive_generate" "multi_call". approximate Logical. TRUE label partial coverage (unresolved tokens). relevant adaptive_generate; always FALSE multi_call. coverage Named numeric vector. Per-label fraction tokens scored (0.0 1.0). 1.0 = fully resolved. n_calls Integer. Number API calls made. raw_response List. Raw response data debugging.

title: Classification Result — classification_result
text: list class classification_result components: prediction Character. predicted label. confidence Numeric. Confidence score (0-1). probabilities Named numeric vector. Probability distribution. method Character. Scoring method used. approximate Logical. Whether scores approximate. coverage Named numeric vector. Per-label token coverage. n_calls Integer. Number API calls. raw_response List. Raw API response debugging.

title: Classification Result — classification_result
code: res <- classification_result(   prediction = "positive",   confidence = 0.85,   probabilities = c(positive = 0.85, negative = 0.10, neutral = 0.05) ) print(res$prediction) #> [1] "positive"

title: Generate a constrained classification — generate
text: Generic hierarchical constrained generation. Makes 1 max_calls constrained API calls. first call constrains model labels produces internally consistent probability distribution. Supplementary calls (max_calls > 1) resolve label clusters reproportioning probability mass within cluster — never change -group totals, accuracy degrade call budget grows.

title: Generate a constrained classification — generate
code: generate(classifier, text, choices, system_prompt = NULL, ..., max_calls = 1L)

title: Generate a constrained classification — generate
text: classifier classifier object created llm_classifier(). text Character. text classify. choices Either character vector labels named list mapping labels descriptions. system_prompt Character NULL. Optional custom system prompt. ... Additional arguments (future extensibility). max_calls Integer NULL. Maximum number API calls. 1 = single call, cluster resolution (default). K = adaptive resolution K calls. NULL = resolve clusters recursively.

text: classification_result() list.

Results memoized per label.

title: Unified LLM Classifier — llm_classifier
text: Create backend-agnostic classifier two confidence scoring methods: generate(): Hierarchical constrained generation. single constrained call produces probability distribution labels using divergence-aware logprobs winning path. max_calls > 1, supplementary calls resolve clusters labels share token prefix diverge winner — reproportion probability mass within cluster, never changing -group totals. guarantees accuracy never degrades call budget grows. classify(): Multi-call completion scoring geometric-mean normalization. Always exact. Makes N calls N labels.

title: Ollama API Client Helpers — ollama-api

title: Ollama API Client Helpers — ollama-api
text: Modern Ollama (>=v0.12) removed /api/tokenize endpoint support fill---middle ("insert") instruct models. module therefore obtains label tokenization completion scores empirical forced constrained generation: forces label valid choice chat() call reads back model's genuine per-token logprobs. /api/tokenize suffix/insert calls used.

title: Ollama Backend — ollama_backend
text: Backend Ollama runtime (>=v0.12) via native Ollama REST API. Ollama uses JSON Schema enum label constraints. model generates {"label": "<chosen>"}. Structural JSON tokens filtered trie reconstruction completion scoring. Modern Ollama removed /api/tokenize endpoint support fill---middle ("insert") instruct models. backend therefore obtains label tokenization completion scores empirical forced constrained generation (forcing label valid choice reading back model's genuine per-token logprobs). /api/tokenize suffix/insert calls used. Tokenization results memoized per label.

what: rollama: Classify Text with LLMs on Ollama and Other Backends — rollama
title: rollama: Classify Text with LLMs on Ollama and Other Backends — rollama
text: wrapper around Ollama REST API inference engines (vLLM, SGLang, llama.cpp) text classification constrained output confidence scoring. backends use empirical forced constrained generation tokenization echo/prefill forced generation completion scoring. Provides two scoring methods: generate(): Adaptive constrained generation divergence-aware confidence scoring, budget-controlled via max_calls. classify(): Multi-call completion scoring geometric-mean normalization. Gold-standard accuracy.

title: rollama: Classify Text with LLMs on Ollama and Other Backends — rollama
text: Adaptive constrained generation trie-based confidence scoring Multi-call completion scoring geometric-mean normalization Eliminates confidence concentration bias raw logprob sums Support multiple inference backends: Ollama, vLLM, SGLang, llama.cpp Batch processing multiple texts Support simple labels labels descriptions

title: rollama: Classify Text with LLMs on Ollama and Other Backends — rollama
code: backend <- ollama_backend("llama3.2") classifier <- llm_classifier(backend)  result <- classify(   classifier,   text = "I love this product!",   choices = c("positive", "negative", "neutral") ) print(result$prediction) print(result$confidence)

title: SGLang Backend — sglang_backend
text: Backend SGLang inference server. Uses regex constraint bare-label generation, producing clean label text JSON wrapper. score() uses echo/prefill (/v1/completions echo=TRUE) recover genuine per-label logprobs. tokenize() uses forced constrained generation via regex token boundaries match actual constrained-generation output. Results memoized per label.

title: vLLM Backend — vllm_backend
text: Backend vLLM inference server. vLLM provides high-throughput serving engine OpenAI-compatible API. supports structured_outputs.choice (vLLM v0.12.0+) bare-label constrained generation, generating bare label text JSON wrapper. score() uses echo/prefill (/v1/completions echo=TRUE) recover genuine per-label logprobs. tokenize() uses forced constrained generation token boundaries match actual constrained-generation output. Results memoized per label.
